medications: Topical diclofenac gel (as needed for knee/hand pain)
health.mental_health: Generally resilient but experiences periodic anxiety and frustration related to chronic pain, reduced vision, and repeated negotiations for workplace accommodations; uses breathing exercises and occasional short-term counseling.
recent_visits: Ophthalmology follow-up at Hospital das Clínicas for intraocular pressure check (last month)
Rheumatology consult for hand and knee osteoarthritis flare (two months ago)
Physical therapy reassessment for mobility and joint-strength plan (three weeks ago)
Primary care visit for blood pressure monitoring and medication renewal (six weeks ago)

finance.income_range: R$6,500–R$9,500 per month (mix of part-time salary and retirement/pension benefits)
finance.debt: Carrying a small personal loan used to cover out-of-pocket eye drops, assistive tech, and a home safety upgrade; actively paying it down monthly.
recent_transactions: Purchased prescription progressive lenses and glare-reducing coating (optical shop in Vila Mariana)
Paid for a screen reader subscription and larger high-contrast monitor for home office
Monthly payment to physical therapy clinic copay
Transfer to a sibling for shared caregiving support for an older relative (private family arrangement)

relationships: Close friend: Renata Almeida (neighbor in Vila Mariana who helps with errands during flare-ups)
Sibling: Arjun Patel (lives in Campinas; coordinates family logistics and occasional financial support)
Work ally: Camila Soares (HR manager who supports accommodation requests)
Community contact: Padre Miguel (local community center organizer who connects Quinn to accessibility groups)
social.social_media_activity: Uses Facebook and WhatsApp groups focused on accessibility rights in São Paulo; posts occasionally about inclusive workplace practices and shares event notices, but keeps personal health details mostly private.
private_conversations: Discussing legal options and documentation for workplace accommodations after a denied request
Talking with friends about fears of losing independence due to worsening vision
Confidential discussions with sibling about budgeting for long-term care and assistive devices

schedule.work_hours: Monday–Thursday, 10:00–16:00 (hybrid); Fridays reserved for medical appointments and administrative tasks
regular_appointments: Physical therapy twice weekly (Tuesday and Thursday mornings)
Monthly blood pressure check at a local clinic/pharmacy
Weekly accessibility advocacy meeting (online, Wednesday evening)
upcoming_events: Union/employee committee meeting about inclusive hiring and accommodations (next week)
Follow-up eye exam with visual field testing (in three weeks)
Family visit to Campinas to see sibling (next month)
schedule.daily_routine: Starts with eye drops and stretching; works mid-day with frequent breaks for joint stiffness and screen fatigue; uses public transit or ride-hailing depending on pain/vision; evenings include short walks when safe, meal prep, and voice-to-text journaling.

professional.company: Mid-sized healthcare services company with clinics across São Paulo (administrative office near Avenida Paulista)
projects: Updating the reasonable accommodations workflow and manager training materials
Reviewing job descriptions for essential functions and accessibility barriers
Coordinating annual compliance refresher on discrimination and harassment policies
Piloting accessible onboarding materials (large print, high-contrast PDFs, screen-reader compatible forms)
professional.performance_issues: Occasional delays in document review and form processing during osteoarthritis flare-ups and after eye appointments; has requested flexible deadlines and improved accessibility of internal HR systems.
workplace_relationships: Supportive relationship with HR manager Camila Soares, who champions inclusive practices
Tension with an IT lead who deprioritized accessibility fixes for the HR portal
Collaborative partnership with Legal on accommodation documentation and risk management

hobbies: Gentle water aerobics when joints allow
Birdwatching in Ibirapuera Park using a monocular on good-vision days
Attending talks at cultural centers (when seating and lighting are accessible)
habits: Uses a cane on bad pain days and requests priority seating on transit
Keeps home lighting high-contrast and labeled for low-vision navigation
Tracks medication times with voice reminders to avoid missed eye drops
Avoids driving at night due to glare and reduced peripheral vision
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in an elevator building in Vila Mariana with accessibility modifications (grab bars, non-slip mats, brighter hallway lighting) and actively advocates with the condo association for safer common-area lighting and clear signage.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Moderates salt for blood pressure; prioritizes high-fiber foods, vegetables, and fish; limits alcohol; stays hydrated to reduce eye dryness and manages weight to ease joint pain.
